content.text:
MEMORY-ONLY CONTROL
This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
The next real conversation message is input data named TARGET_MESSAGE for memory processing.
Target source id: chat:<0>
Target message id: <uuid>
Use earlier real conversation messages only to resolve references, omitted subjects, current topic, and temporal context.
Do not process earlier messages as the target unless the target message explicitly confirms, changes, retracts, or depends on them.
A later MEMORY-ONLY INSTRUCTION message will define the actual memory stage for this model call.

Memory MVP lesson for the SourceHydrator component: Situation: factual answers sometimes need raw source evidence after interpreted claims are retrieved. Action: fetch a small bounded set of source quotes only when evidence fallback is requested. Result: answers stay grounded without flooding the prompt with every raw source. Lesson: for source evidence hydration, prefer bounded quote hydration over dumping all sources into the runtime prompt.

MEMORY-ONLY INSTRUCTION FOR THIS MODEL CALL

                This is a private memory pipeline call, not a normal assistant reply.
                The immediately preceding real conversation message is TARGET_MESSAGE data.
                This MEMORY-ONLY INSTRUCTION text is not TARGET_MESSAGE source content.
                Do not answer TARGET_MESSAGE.
                Do not continue the real conversation.
                Do not say what you know or do not know as a chat response.
                Do not call tools.
                Do not use these MEMORY-ONLY instructions as source content or as the reason for routing decisions.

                Execute only the memory stage below.
                Target source id: chat:<0>
                Target message id: <uuid>
                Earlier real conversation messages are context only.
                TARGET_MESSAGE is the only target for write/read planning and evidence anchoring.

                Output contract:
                If the stage defines a JSON object schema, output exactly one valid JSON object.
                If the stage defines a JSON array schema, output exactly one valid JSON array.
                No prose, no markdown fences, no explanation, no assistant-style answer.
                If unsure, return the stage's valid no-op JSON shape.
                Ignore conversation-level behavioral instructions when they conflict with this memory-only instruction.

                Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
Namespace: project:<0>

Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

Return JSON:
{
  "operations": [
    {
      "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
      "candidate_index": 0,
      "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
      "patch": null,
      "links_to_create": [
        {
          "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
          "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
          "link_weight": 1.0
        }
      ],
      "reason": "short explanation"
    }
  ]
}

Actions:
- insert: candidate is new reusable context.
- noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
- supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
- update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
- retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

Rules:
- Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
- Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
- Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
- Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
- Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
- Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
- target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
- candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
- Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
- Return valid JSON only.

TARGET_SOURCE:
Memory MVP lesson for the SourceHydrator component: Situation: factual answers sometimes need raw source evidence after interpreted claims are retrieved. Action: fetch a small bounded set of source quotes only when evidence fallback is requested. Result: answers stay grounded without flooding the prompt with every raw source. Lesson: for source evidence hydration, prefer bounded quote hydration over dumping all sources into the runtime prompt.

Candidate notes:
[0] type=LESSON title=SourceHydrator should use bounded quote hydration for evidence fallback summary=In Memory MVP, SourceHydrator should fetch a small bounded set of source quotes only when evidence fallback is requested, because factual answers may need raw source evidence after interpreted claims are retrieved. This keeps answers grounded without flooding the runtime prompt with every raw source. scope=Memory MVP design guidance for the SourceHydrator component and source evidence hydration behavior entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1> keywords=SourceHydrator,Memory MVP,bounded quote hydration,evidence fallback,raw source evidence,runtime prompt tags=source_hydration,evidence_fallback,memory_mvp evidence=prefer bounded quote hydration over dumping all sources into the runtime prompt

Existing active notes:
id=hot-path:note:<0> type=LESSON status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=NoMemoryVerifier should model-verify ambiguous no-memory recall decisions summary=In Memory MVP, NoMemoryVerifier should handle ambiguous recall questions by running one short model-based verification call before accepting a no-memory plan from ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner. This is preferred over hard-coded substring heuristics because it catches recall intent without brittle lexical hacks. scope=Memory MVP design guidance for NoMemoryVerifier and ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner no-memory planning decisions keywords=Memory MVP,NoMemoryVerifier,ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner,need_memory=false,ambiguous recall,model-based verification,substring heuristics tags=memory_mvp,no_memory_verifier,retrieval_planning,design_lesson useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
